executive_summary: The Iran Hormuz blockade is the single dominant event — oil above $106/barrel, deadlocked diplomacy, and a May 1 War Powers Act deadline forcing a decision. NATO alliance fractures are going public (Spain suspension floated). EU unlocked $106B for Ukraine after Hungary relented. Meanwhile, US inflation is called 'worst in 4 years' — stagflation risk rising — and Meta cut 10% of jobs as AI restructuring accelerates. Climate is in simultaneous multi-crisis: GA/FL wildfires (87 homes destroyed), Super El Niño risk flagged for 2026, and WMO declaring Earth's climate 'more unbalanced than ever.' The cross-domain pattern is convergence: war + inflation + climate are compounding simultaneously, with no single authority positioned to manage all three.

key_themes.theme: Hormuz Blockade — Economic War of Attrition
key_themes.description: The US-Iran standoff has settled into a protracted blockade at the Strait of Hormuz (20% of global oil flow). Pakistan is mediating, but Iran is following a Vietnam-style protracted resistance playbook. Trump warns Americans to expect high gas prices 'for a little while.' May 1 War Powers Act deadline looms — 60 days without congressional approval.
supporting_evidence: Brent tops $106/bbl — first sustained $100+ since 2022
Pakistan maintaining security lockdown while pushing diplomacy
Foreign Policy: 'Iran is calling the shots now'
Trump: 'higher gas prices for a little while'

key_themes.description: The Pentagon floated suspending Spain from NATO over the Iran rift — an extraordinary public disclosure of alliance fracture. Poland separately questioned US reliability. The credibility of the US security guarantee is being tested simultaneously in Europe (Ukraine) and the Indo-Pacific (Japan building southern shield independently).
supporting_evidence: Pentagon email: options to suspend Spain from NATO
Japan: 'most severe security environment since 1945' — building autonomous southern shield
Foreign Policy: 'The Quad is on the brink of extinction'
Hungary's veto leverage on Ukraine aid now spent — EU $106B unlocked

key_themes.description: Oil above $100, US inflation called 'worst in nearly 4 years,' and the Fed facing a FOMC decision next week with almost no room to ease. JPMorgan warning on risk positioning. Avis shares collapsed 57% in two days — a warning on crowded momentum trades. TSMC at record high (AI demand intact) but market breadth is narrowing.
supporting_evidence: MarketWatch: US inflation 'worst in almost 4 years'
JPMorgan: blunt warning to investors shifting into risk assets
Fed finalizing community bank rules; FOMC next week
Intel +20% on earnings beat — US govt holds $26.5B unrealized gain on stake

key_themes.description: No less than 6 concurrent climate extremes: GA/FL wildfires (87 homes destroyed), Oregon drought emergency (6 counties), Afghanistan water crisis, Texas Panhandle wildfire warning, Colorado CSU campus evacuation, and WMO's starkest warning yet. Super El Niño risk flagged for 2026 could amplify all of the above.
supporting_evidence: WMO: 'Earth's climate more unbalanced than ever'
Super El Niño could trigger extreme global weather in 2026
Georgia/Florida: 87 homes destroyed, extreme drought fueling fires
Oregon: record-warm winter caused snowpack loss — emergency in 6 counties

key_themes.theme: AI Restructuring Accelerating — Big Tech Labor Reallocation
key_themes.description: Meta cutting 10% of workforce (~8,000 jobs) to fund AI investment. Nike cut 1,400. This is a sector-wide reallocation from human labor to AI infrastructure. Meanwhile DeepSeek V4-Pro outperforms all open-source rivals as the US moves to restrict Chinese AI — the tech rivalry is entering a new phase.
supporting_evidence: Meta: 10% workforce cut, ~8,000 jobs
DeepSeek V4-Pro outperforms all rival open models
Trump admin moving to restrict Chinese AI firms
US-China AI rivalry escalating

alpha_opportunities.angle: Oil tanker demand surge from prolonged Hormuz disruption
alpha_opportunities.rationale: Suezmax/Aframax rates spike when Strait is disrupted. Andurand Capital lost 52% on ceasefire — but blockade resuming after talks fail

alpha_opportunities.angle: Japan defense buildup — autonomous military industrial capacity
alpha_opportunities.rationale: Japan building southern shield independently means Mitsubishi Heavy, Kawasaki Heavy, and defense subcontractors benefit from multi-year rearmament cycle
Morgan Stanley positioning as stablecoin reserve manager, Metaplanet issuing zero-coupon bonds for more BTC, Saylor calling 'winter over'

alpha_opportunities.angle: Private credit systemic risk — short exposure via credit default swaps
alpha_opportunities.rationale: ECB and JPMorgan both flagged shadow banking/private credit interconnecting with equity markets as growing systemic risk

cross_domain_patterns: Hormuz oil shock + stagflation + climate extremes = compound cost-of-living crisis hitting simultaneously
US credibility as security guarantor questioned in both Europe (NATO) and Asia (Japan/Quad) simultaneously
War finance: EU $106B Ukraine loan, US $75B FY2027 military AI request, Japan defense buildup — all spending simultaneously
Climate-war convergence: Afghanistan water crisis, Gaza humanitarian gap, and Texas/Georgia disasters all unreported relative to geopolitical noise
blind_spots: Gaza death toll (72,568+) — largely absent from Western political discourse while ceasefire calculations continue
FISA 702 April 30 deadline — no public clarity on what authorities have actually been used for in the Iran conflict
Pakistan's 'security lockdown' — human rights implications and stability risk in a nuclear-armed state
ReliefWeb DNS failure — humanitarian disaster reporting from conflict zones is unavailable this cycle
Southern Hemisphere fire season and cyclone activity not represented (autumn there)

reference_index.name: MarketWatch — US Inflation Worst in 4 Years
